description: Evaluates the skill's ability to deploy secure, isolated OpenClaw instances for personal use across different platforms and configurations

evals.name: personal-deploy-telegram
evals.prompt: Deploy OpenClaw for personal use — my-bot telegram
evals.expected_output: Complete project scaffold with Telegram adapter, Docker isolation, hardened settings.json, pre/post tool-use hooks, CLAUDE.md with deny-by-default policy, and verification checks
expectations: Scaffolds project directory with all required files
Creates Dockerfile with multi-stage build and non-root user
Generates docker-compose.yml with security_opt, cap_drop, read_only
Produces settings.json with minimal allowed-tools and hook registrations
Creates .env.example with all required environment variables documented

evals.name: personal-deploy-http
evals.prompt: Set up a secure personal AI assistant with HTTP gateway called home-agent
evals.expected_output: Complete deployment with HTTP adapter, port 3000 bound to 127.0.0.1 only, all security hardening defaults applied
expectations: Recognizes natural language trigger for personal deployment
Creates HTTP adapter exposing port on localhost only
Generates Docker network with internal:true
Includes API key authentication for HTTP endpoints
Produces verification script that tests the deployment

evals.name: personal-deploy-slack
evals.prompt: Create my own Claude agent on Slack — work-assistant slack
evals.expected_output: Complete deployment with Slack adapter, SLACK_BOT_TOKEN and SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET in .env.example, all security artifacts generated
expectations: Creates Slack-specific adapter configuration
Includes Slack-specific env vars in .env.example
Generates workspace-based sender allowlist
Produces all security artifacts matching the kit workflow
Passes the built-in verification script

evals.name: personal-deploy-minimal-input
evals.expected_output: Applies defaults (project: my-openclaw, platform: telegram), generates full deployment with all security controls
expectations: Activates with minimal input using sensible defaults
Creates complete project structure without requiring additional input
Applies all security defaults from the hardening checklist
Generates Docker isolation with maximum restrictions
Produces audit logging hooks

evals.name: false-positive-wifi-security
evals.prompt: How do I secure my home WiFi network and set up a firewall?
evals.expected_output: Skill should NOT activate — this is about network security, not OpenClaw agent deployment
expectations: Does not scaffold a project or generate deployment files
Does not reference OpenClaw, Docker, or agent configuration
Recognizes this as a network security question outside skill scope

evals.name: edge-no-docker-available
evals.prompt: Deploy OpenClaw for personal use but I don't have Docker installed
evals.expected_output: Detects missing Docker in prerequisites, warns about reduced isolation, falls back to non-Docker deployment with OS-level restrictions and security warnings
expectations: Checks for Docker availability in prerequisites phase
Warns prominently about reduced isolation guarantees
Falls back to process-level isolation with hook-based security
Adds extra PreToolUse restrictions to compensate for missing container
Documents accepted risk in the deployment configuration
